summary:
Claim: One-way ANOVA is appropriate to compare mean peak expiratory flow across four distinct smoking groups.  
Evidence: The data involves comparing means across more than two independent groups; the groups are naturally defined (non-smokers, light, moderate, heavy smokers).  
Answer: A  

This test effectively evaluates differences among group means while controlling family-wise error rates—critical when assessing multiple comparisons in clinical research settings. Unlike repeated t-tests, it avoids inflating Type I errors due to multiple hypothesis testing. Given these features, one-way ANOVA remains statistically sound and methodologically preferred here.
